question.text: A partnership firm has a total income of Rs. 1,50,00,000 in FY 2025-26. What is its effective tax rate including surcharge and cess?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For a partnership firm with income exceeding Rs. 1 crore, the tax rate is 30%. Surcharge of 12% applies on tax when income exceeds Rs. 1 crore. Health & Education Cess = 4% on (tax + surcharge). Effective rate = 30% × (1 + 0.12) × (1 + 0.04) = 30% × 1.12 × 1.04 = 34.944%. **The Trap:** 31.20% applies only cess without surcharge (30% × 1.04). 30% ignores both surcharge and cess. 42.744% is the Maximum Marginal Rate for individuals (not firms) using the highest surcharge of 37%. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A tests entity-level taxation when advising on business structuring. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Firms face a flat 30% rate regardless of income quantum (unlike individuals with progressive slabs), but surcharge kicks in above Rs. 1 crore — a critical consideration for firm partners evaluating their effective tax burden.
